lede: A look at December 2 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Learner Tien and Alfred Enoch, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Cannabis (drug)
events.desc: Cannabis is removed from the list of most dangerous drugs of the international drug control treaty by the UN Commission on Narcotic Drugs.

events.title: Ghost Ship warehouse fire
events.desc: Thirty-six people die in a fire at a converted Oakland, California, warehouse serving as an artist collective.

events.title: Hat Yai Peace Agreement
events.desc: The Peace Agreement of Hat Yai is signed and ratified by the Malayan Communist Party (MCP) and the governments of Malaysia and Thailand, ending the over two-decade-long communist insurgency in Malaysia.

events.title: Benazir Bhutto
events.desc: Benazir Bhutto is sworn in as Prime Minister of Pakistan, becoming the first woman to head the government of a Muslim-majority state.

events.title: Wien Consolidated Airlines Flight 55
events.desc: Wien Consolidated Airlines Flight 55 crashes into Pedro Bay, Alaska, killing all 39 people on board.

events.title: Korean War
events.desc: Korean War: The Battle of the Ch'ongch'on River ends with a decisive Chinese victory and UN forces are completely expelled from North Korea.

events.title: Air raid on Bari
events.desc: World War II: A Luftwaffe bombing raid on the harbour of Bari, Italy, sinks numerous cargo and transport ships, including the American SS John Harvey, which is carrying a stockpile of mustard gas.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: During the Manhattan Project, a team led by Enrico Fermi initiates the first artificial self-sustaining nuclear chain reaction.

events.title: Philippine–American War
events.desc: Philippine–American War: The Battle of Tirad Pass, known as the "Filipino Thermopylae", is fought.

events.title: Franz Joseph I
events.desc: Franz Joseph I becomes Emperor of Austria.

births.name: Tom McGuinness (musician)
births.line: English guitarist, songwriter, author, and producer

births.name: Erima Northcroft
births.line: New Zealand soldier, lawyer, and judge (died 1953)

deaths.text: Tom Hendry, Canadian playwright, co-founded the Manitoba Theatre Centre
Wallace Harrison, American architect, co-founded Harrison & Abramovitz

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Maria Angela Astorch
